QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_69

question.text: On death of the minor child in NPS Vatsalya, what happens to the corpus?

answer.text: Entire corpus is paid to the guardian
80% annuity and 20% lump sum to guardian

explanation.text: **The Logic:** On death of the minor child, the entire accumulated corpus in NPS Vatsalya is paid to the guardian as a lump sum. **The Trap:** There is no annuity requirement on death of the child. The entire corpus goes to the guardian, similar to All Citizens Model death benefit. **Cross-Exam Context:** Death of child: entire corpus to guardian. Death of guardian: new guardian through fresh KYC process, account continues. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Ensure guardians understand that the Vatsalya account continues if the guardian dies, with a new guardian appointed through fresh KYC.
